title: subagent: run_subagent is a 311-line god function with deeply nested branches
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/subagent.rs:657-968 (run_subagent)
## Violation
`run_subagent` spans lines 658-968 (311 lines), with `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` masking the breach. The function intermixes:

1. Resume-vs-fresh transcript bootstrapping (lines 664-702)
2. Optional worktree creation and system-prompt injection (lines 705-731)
3. Tool definition filtering (lines 733-750)
4. Model resolution (lines 752-763)
5. Provider URL/key extraction (lines 769-780)
6. Permission manager construction (lines 789-793)
7. The agent turn loop containing the API call, response parsing, content extraction, tool-call extraction, tool dispatch and result formatting (lines 795-944)
8. Finalization, transcript storage, worktree cleanup (lines 947-967)

Each block has independent reasons to change (SRP). Cyclomatic complexity exceeds the framework's V(G) > 15 reject threshold; the inner content-extraction block at lines 859-876 alone is depth-5 nested. Five early-return error sites each rebuild the same `SubagentResult { agent_id, success: false, ... }` literal (DRY).

## Mandated refactor
Extract:
- `prepare_messages(config) -> Result<(AgentId, Vec<Value>)>` (handles resume and fresh init)
- `prepare_worktree(config, agent_id) -> Result<Option<WorktreeIsolation>>`
- `pick_model(config, app_config) -> String`
- `extract_final_text(assistant_message) -> Option<String>`
- a helper `fail_subagent(agent_id, msg, ...) -> SubagentResult` for the 5 duplicated bail-out literals
- A top-level `agent_loop(state) -> Result<String, String>` containing only the iteration

Remove `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` once refactored.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
